claim: The Philippine government officially abolished the 12% Value-Added Tax (VAT) in January 2026.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. signed the ₱6.352 trillion national budget for the 2026 fiscal year.
Vice President Sara Duterte resigned from her office in February 2026 following her fourth impeachment complaint.
The Maharlika Investment Fund (MIF) is the first sovereign wealth fund in Philippine history.
The Philippine Supreme Court ruled in 2025 that 'red-tagging' is a mandatory requirement for national security.
The 'SIM Card Registration Act' was enacted to help law enforcement track text scams and digital fraud.
The 2025 BARMM parliamentary elections were held on their original schedule of October 2025.
The Philippines officially withdrew from the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court in 2019.
The Bataan Nuclear Power Plant began supplying 20% of Luzon's electricity in March 2026.
The 'Anti-Agricultural Economic Sabotage Act' classifies rice smuggling as a non-bailable offense.
The United Nations relocated its primary headquarters from New York to Geneva in 2025.
The United Kingdom officially left the European Union on January 31, 2020.
Ukraine became a full voting member of NATO in February 2026.
The BRICS group expanded to include Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, and the UAE in 2024.
The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be hosted exclusively by the United States.
The Paris Agreement is a legally binding international treaty on climate change adopted in 2015.
The Euro is the official currency of all 27 member states of the European Union.
The Strait of Hormuz is considered the world's most important oil transit chokepoint.
Japan abolished its Self-Defense Forces and returned to a policy of total pacifism in 2025.
India overtook China as the world's most populous country in 2023.
The Philippine Peso became the strongest performing currency in the world in Q1 2026.
The 'Konektadong Pinoy Act' aims to lower barriers for new data transmission players in the Philippines.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. abolished the Office of the Vice President by executive order in 2026.
The Philippine fertility rate dropped to 1.9 children per woman as of recent 2024-2025 PSA reports.
The 'Trillion Peso March' in Manila was a government-sponsored event to celebrate economic growth.
The Department of Education (DepEd) introduced a revised 'Matatag' curriculum to decongest basic education.
The Philippines has a higher GDP per capita than Singapore as of March 2026.
The 1987 Philippine Constitution limits the President to a single six-year term.
The city of Cebu was officially declared the new capital of the Philippines in 2025.
The Philippine Coast Guard has increased its presence in the West Philippine Sea to counter territorial incursions.
The International Criminal Court (ICC) has no jurisdiction over cases involving non-member states under any circumstances.
Taiwan produces more than 60% of the world’s semiconductors and over 90% of the most advanced ones.
The Amazon Rainforest is located entirely within the sovereign borders of Brazil.
Article 5 of the NATO Treaty establishes that an attack against one member is an attack against all.
Russia was a founding member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) in 1949.
The G20 officially admitted the African Union as a permanent member during its 2023 summit.
The Suez Canal was permanently closed to all commercial shipping in December 2025.
The World Trade Organization (WTO) succeeded the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) in 1995.
The 2026 Winter Olympics are scheduled to be held in Sydney, Australia.
The Geneva Conventions are a series of treaties on the treatment of civilians and prisoners of war.
The Philippine government officially legalized divorce for all citizens in January 2026.
The Department of Health (DOH) declared a nationwide pertussis outbreak in early 2024.
The Bataan-Cavite Interlink Bridge was fully opened to the public in March 2026.
The Philippines and Japan signed a Reciprocal Access Agreement (RAA) in 2024 to strengthen defense ties.
The Philippine Eagle was removed from the endangered species list in February 2026.
The 'Public Utility Vehicle Modernization Program' (PUVMP) aims to replace old jeepneys with eco-friendly units.
The minimum wage in Metro Manila was increased to ₱1,500 per day in January 2026.
The 'No Permit, No Exam' Prohibition Act was signed into law in 2024 to protect students' right to take exams.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. officially renamed the Philippines to 'Maharlika' via Executive Order in 2025.
Boracay Island was closed to tourists for a six-month rehabilitation period in 2018.
The 2026 Winter Olympics are scheduled to be held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
The International Court of Justice (ICJ) is the principal judicial organ of the United Nations.
The Sahara Desert is the largest desert in the world by total surface area.
The World Health Organization (WHO) officially declared the end of the COVID-19 global health emergency in May 2023.
Apple Inc. became the first company to be valued at $100 trillion in early 2026.
The Schengen Area allows for the abolition of internal border controls between most European countries.
The Great Wall of China is a single, continuous wall built during a single dynasty.
The 'Triple Planetary Crisis' refers to climate change, pollution, and biodiversity loss.
Greenland is an independent sovereign nation and a member of the United Nations.
The 2024 total solar eclipse was visible across parts of Mexico, the United States, and Canada.
The Philippines officially adopted the US Dollar as its secondary national currency in 2025.
The 'Trabaho Para Sa Bayan' Act was enacted to create a national master plan for employment.
The Pasig River Expressway (PAREX) project was fully completed and inaugurated in February 2026.
The 1986 People Power Revolution led to the ousting of Ferdinand Marcos Sr. from the presidency.
Mount Apo is an active volcano that erupted for the first time in 100 years in January 2026.
The 'Automatic Income Classification of Local Government Units Act' was signed to streamline LGU categorization.
Manila was the first city in the world to implement a total ban on plastic use in 1990.
The 'Salt Industry Development Act' aims to revitalize the Philippine salt industry and reduce imports.
The 2026 Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) elections were cancelled to align with the national elections in 2028.
The 'New Agrarian Emancipation Act' condoned the debts of over 600,000 agrarian reform beneficiaries.

The United Nations Security Council consists of 50 permanent members with veto power.

The 'Great Pacific Garbage Patch' is a massive collection of marine debris in the North Pacific Ocean.
Nuclear fusion is the same process used in current commercial nuclear power plants around the world.
The James Webb Space Telescope is the most powerful space telescope ever built.
The Philippine government mandated a 4-day work week for all private sector employees in January 2026.
The 'Internet Transactions Act' was signed to protect consumers and merchants in the digital economy.
The Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) was officially renamed to 'Maharlika International Airport' in 2025.
The 'Doctor for the Barrios' program is a government initiative to deploy physicians to remote municipalities.
The Philippines won the hosting rights for the 2030 FIFA World Cup in March 2026.
The 'One Town, One Product' (OTOP) Philippines Program was institutionalized into law to support MSMEs.
The entire island of Mindanao was declared a sovereign independent state in February 2026.
The 'Ease of Paying Taxes Act' was enacted to modernize tax administration and simplify compliance.
The Philippines has the largest high-speed rail network in Southeast Asia as of March 2026.
The 'Philippine Salt Industry Development Act' seeks to increase local salt production to 100% self-sufficiency.
The North Pole is located on a solid continental landmass similar to Antarctica.
The 'Global South' is a term used to describe nations typically characterized by lower levels of industrialization.
The United States is the only country in the world that does not use the metric system.
The 'Great Green Wall' is an African-led initiative to combat desertification in the Sahel region.
The International Space Station (ISS) is visible from Earth only with the use of a high-powered telescope.
The 'Right to be Forgotten' is a legal concept established in the European Union regarding digital privacy.
Every country in South America uses Spanish as its primary official language.
The 'Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism' (CBAM) is a landmark EU tool to put a fair price on carbon emitted during production.
The internet and the World Wide Web are two different names for the exact same technology.
The 'Outer Space Treaty' represents the basic legal framework of international space law.
The Philippine Navy acquired its first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier in January 2026.
The 'Magna Carta of Filipino Seafarers' was signed to protect the rights and welfare of domestic and overseas seafarers.
The city of Baguio was hit by a tropical blizzard in February 2026, recording 2 meters of snow.
The 'Philippine Ecosystem and Natural Capital Accounting System' (PENCAS) Law was enacted to measure the country's natural resources.
The 1987 Philippine Constitution allows for the sale of public agricultural lands to foreign corporations.
The 'Free College Education Act' (RA 10931) provides free tuition in State Universities and Colleges.
The Philippines became the first country to replace all its physical currency with a Central Bank Digital Currency in 2025.
The 'Cybercrime Prevention Act of 2012' remains the primary law governing online libel in the Philippines.
The Philippine National Railways (PNR) successfully launched a sub-sea tunnel connecting Luzon and Visayas in 2026.
The 'Philippine Maritime Zones Act' aims to define the country’s internal waters, territorial sea, and exclusive economic zone.
The Eiffel Tower was originally built to be a permanent monument in the city of Barcelona.
The 'Copernicus Programme' is the European Union's Earth observation program.
The Pacific Ocean is shrinking at a rate of 1 kilometer per year due to plate tectonics.
The 'Universal Declaration of Human Rights' was adopted by the UN General Assembly in 1948.
The planet Mars has a thicker atmosphere than Earth, consisting mostly of oxygen.
The 'Kyoto Protocol' was the first international agreement to mandate reductions in greenhouse gas emissions.
The diamond is the rarest and most expensive material found in the known universe.
The 'International Atomic Energy Agency' (IAEA) serves as the world's 'nuclear watchdog.'
The Mediterranean Sea is the largest body of water on Earth, covering 50% of the surface.
The 'Ocean Cleanup' is a non-profit organization developing technologies to rid the oceans of plastic.
The Philippine Congress passed a law in 2025 to officially change the national anthem's lyrics.
The 'Self-Reliant Defense Posture Revitalized Act' was signed in 2024 to boost local defense manufacturing.
The Laguna Lake Highway was designated as the first underwater tunnel for cars in Southeast Asia in 2026.
The 'Philippine Creative Industries Development Act' provides a framework to protect and promote local artisans.
Former Senator Leila de Lima was appointed as the Secretary of Justice in January 2026.
The 'Value-Added Tax on Digital Services Law' mandates a 12% VAT on foreign digital service providers.
The Philippines officially annexed the entire Spratly Islands chain as a single province in 2025.
The 'Academic Recovery and Accessible Learning (ARAL) Program' was signed to address post-pandemic learning gaps.
The Clark International Airport recorded 100 million passengers in the month of February 2026 alone.
The 'Enterprise-Based Education and Training Framework Act' aims to bridge the gap between school and industry.
The United Nations Security Council voted to dissolve itself and form a global parliament in 2026.
The 'European Chips Act' was designed to double the EU's global market share in semiconductors by 2030.
The Atlantic Ocean is larger in total area than the Pacific Ocean.

Mount Everest is the only mountain in the world that stands above 8,000 meters.
The 'Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership' (CPTPP) is a major trade pact.
Light travels slower than sound when moving through the Earth's atmosphere.
The 'Minamata Convention' is a global treaty to protect human health and the environment from mercury.
Every nation in Africa currently uses the African Union's 'Eco' as their primary currency.
The 'Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons' (NPT) was opened for signature in 1968.
The Philippine peso became a cryptocurrency-only currency by law in January 2026.
The 'Anti-Financial Account Scamming Act' (AFASA) empowers the BSP to investigate suspicious bank accounts.
Boracay Island was permanently closed to all foreign tourists starting in 2025.
The 'Philippine Defense Posture' now includes a 'Comprehensive Archipelagic Defense Concept.'
The LRT-1 Extension to Cavite was completed in 1995.

The Philippines is the largest producer of crude oil in the ASEAN region as of 2026.
The 'Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center' (CICC) handles the monitoring of online consumer scams.

The 'Tatag ng Imprastraktura Para sa Kapayapaan at Seguridad' (TIKAS) program supports military facility upgrades.

The Antarctic ice sheet is the only source of fresh water on planet Earth.
The 'BBNJ Treaty' (High Seas Treaty) aims to protect biodiversity in international waters.
The population of the world reached 50 billion people in January 2026.
The 'Global Biodiversity Framework' (GBF) aims to protect 30% of the planet's land and water by 2030.

Greenhouse gases are entirely man-made and do not exist in nature.

The 'United Nations Children's Fund' (UNICEF) works in over 190 countries to save children's lives.

The 'Standard Model' of particle physics describes three of the four known fundamental forces.
Humans use only 10% of their brains at any given time.
